vitals.entry_fee: 2026: SEK 712 (early bird) to SEK 1,100 (on-site) adults; SEK 356-525 ages 12-14
vitals.feed_zones: Three aid stations: Borghamn (32 km), Rök (55 km), Skänninge (79 km)

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 69. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 66. WAVE 6 (2026-07-24): climbing corrected 3->1 after research sourced total climbing at approximately 325m via a rider GPS trace (organizer publishes no numeric figure) — rubric band 1 ('Flat: <500m'), not band 3 ('Rolling: 1,500-2,500m'). Score recalculated: 66 -> 63.

course_description.character: A flat-to-rolling 100 km loop out of Motala, heading south along Lake Vättern through Vadstena and up the Omberg ridge, then turning inland across the Östergötland plain through Rök and Skänninge before returning to Motala. The route runs alongside the lake but does not circle it — that's the job of the full 315 km Vätternrundan, a separate event run by the same organizer that shares some course infrastructure. Omberg supplies the one real climbing feature on an otherwise gentle course; the organizer itself describes it as challenging but explicitly not a mountain stage. Total climbing is approximately 325 m by a rider-recorded GPS trace — the organizer publishes no official numeric figure.
course_description.signature_challenge: Omberg — the one significant climb on an otherwise flat-to-rolling course, arriving after Vadstena early in the route.
suffering_zones: Omberg ridge — the course's single real climbing test, well before the halfway point
The inland stretch through Rök and Skänninge — long, exposed, flat mileage between the last aid station and the finish, where fatigue rather than terrain does the damage

logistics.lodging_strategy: Information available on official event pages; Motala offers standard accommodation options for event participants.

history.origin_story: Launched in 1991 as a women-only companion event to Vätternrundan Bike Week, held annually in Motala since (all 2020 editions across the Vätternrundan family, including Tjejvättern, were cancelled due to COVID-19). It is a genuinely separate registered event, not a women's category folded into the main Vätternrundan or Halvvättern — it has its own eligibility rules (women only, minimum age 12), start window, and results feed, even though it shares organizer, week, and some course infrastructure with those events.

biased_opinion.verdict: A Genuinely Separate Women-Only Event, Modest Terrain
biased_opinion.summary: TjejVättern is a genuinely separate, women-only 100 km recreational road ride within Vätternrundan Bike Week, not a women's category folded into another Vätternrundan event (Vätternrundan 100 km and 315 km are both mixed-gender, open to all cyclists). The 2026 edition drew 2,131 starters on a flat-to-rolling course whose only notable climb is Omberg. Held since 1991 (with a COVID-related gap in 2020), it sits inside an established Vätternrundan Bike Week. Total climbing is only around 325 m over 100 km, and the route runs alongside Lake Vättern without circling it — that's the separate 315 km Vätternrundan.
strengths: A genuinely separate, women-only event with its own eligibility rules and results feed since 1991 — not a category folded into another Vätternrundan product
Established field (2,131 starters in 2026) with three staffed aid stations and its own results feed
Accessible entry point: minimum age 12, flat-to-rolling 100 km course with one notable climb (Omberg), suited to first-time long-distance riders
Confirmed ongoing — the organizer has already scheduled the next edition for June 2027
weaknesses: Limited climbing difficulty — approximately 325 m over 100 km, with Omberg the only notable climb
Roads remain open to ordinary traffic during the event, per the organizer's own rules — this is not a fully closed-road event
The organizer does not publish an official elevation figure — the ~325 m used here comes from a single rider's GPS trace, not an organizer-verified source

Does not circle Lake Vättern — that's the full 315 km Vätternrundan; this route runs alongside the lake for a stretch, and shares some timing checkpoints with the separately-registered Vätternrundan 100 km, which can make wrong-event footage easy to mistake for this one (documented in this database's own prior YouTube data, since purged)
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A genuinely separate women-only entry point into Vätternrundan Bike Week — a good fit for the event's own community and accessible format, not for a demanding course.

final_verdict.one_liner: A genuinely separate women-only 100 km ride inside Vätternrundan Bike Week — flat-to-rolling, not a physical test.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes, if you want an accessible entry into Swedish mass-participation cycling with a genuine women-only field. Don't expect serious climbing or a full lap of Lake Vättern — that's the separate 315 km Vätternrundan.
final_verdict.alternatives: For the full lake circuit, the 315 km Vätternrundan (same organizer, same week, mixed-gender). For a middle distance, Halvvättern (150 km, also mixed-gender). Both are separately registered events, not variants of this profile.

eligibility.notes: Women-only 100 km recreational road ride in Motala, per the official event page (eligibility, age 12+, active status, next edition June 12, 2027). The 2026 edition was held June 6, 2026, with 2,131 starters / 2,118 finishers per the official results feed (result.vatternrundan.se).

catalog_flags.status_note: Purged the entire youtube_data block this wave (5 videos + rider_intel): every item was verified-wrong-event content describing Vätternrundan (2009/2013/2016/2017 editions, the separate 315km mixed-gender lake circuit) or Halvvättern (2015, the separate 150km event), not TjejVättern itself. TjejVättern, Vätternrundan, and Halvvättern are confirmed as three genuinely separate registered events sharing an organizer and course infrastructure but distinct eligibility/registration/results — this database correctly carries them as three separate profiles (tjejvattern.json, vatternrundan.json, halvvattern.json); no merge or dedupe action needed.
